name: Blackberry Cobbler
recipeIngredient: 6 tbsp Unsalted butter, cold, cut into pieces
4 cups Blackberries, picked over and rinsed
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, stir together the cornstarch and 1/4 cup cold water until the cornstarch is completely dissolved.
2. Add 1 cup sugar, lemon juice, and blackberries to the cornstarch mixture. Combine gently but thoroughly.
3. Transfer the mixture to an 8-inch cast-iron skillet.
4. In a separate bowl, combine the flour, remaining sugar, baking powder, and salt.
5. Blend in the cold butter until the mixture resembles coarse meal.
6. Add 1/4 cup boiling water and stir the mixture until it forms a dough.
7. Bring the blackberry mixture to a boil on top of the stove, stirring occasionally.
8. Drop spoonfuls of the dough carefully into the boiling mixture.
9. Bake the cobbler in a preheated 400°F oven for 20-25 minutes or until the topping is golden brown.
10. Serve warm with vanilla ice cream.
